# Agent detail tools display and selection (2026-01-08 09:50Z)

_**User (2026-01-07 12:48Z)**_

update @src/components/AgentDetail.tsx to list the tools linked to this agent by using this api call instead  

curl -X 'GET' \
  'http://127.0.0.1:8000/agent-tools/agent/3805ca45-6848-49fd-918e-848cc98956fa/tools' \
  -H 'accept: application/json

then, for the dialog box that is opened fetch all the tools that are available similar to @src/app/tools/page.tsx but only show those tools that are not already added to the agent

_**Agent (model claude-4.5-opus-high-thinking, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants me to update the AgentDetail.tsx component to:

1. List the tools linked to an agent using the API endpoint `/agent-tools/agent/{agent_uuid}/tools` instead of the current `/tools` endpoint
2. In the dialog box for adding tools, fetch all available tools but only show those that are NOT already added to the agent

Let me analyze the current code:

1. Currently, the tools are fetched from `${backendUrl}/tools` in a useEffect
2. The same tools list is used both for displaying in the Tools tab and in the Add Tool dialog

I need to:
1. Create a new state to hold the agent's linked tools (fetched from `/agent-tools/agent/{agentUuid}/tools`)
2. Keep the existing tools state for all available tools (or create a new one)
3. In the Add Tool dialog, filter out tools that are already linked to the agent
4. The tools displayed in the Tools tab should come from the agent-specific endpoint

Let me make these changes:

1. Rename the current `tools` state to `allTools` or keep it as `tools` for all tools
2. Add a new state `agentTools` for tools linked to the agent
3. Modify the tools tab to display `agentTools` instead of `tools`
4. Modify the Add Tool dialog to show `allTools.filter(tool => !agentTools.find(at => at.uuid === tool.uuid))`

Let me look at the code more carefully:

Current state variables related to tools:
- `tools` - fetched from `/tools`
- `toolsLoading`
- `toolsError`
- `toolsSearchQuery`
- `addToolDialogOpen`
- `addToolDialogSearchQuery`

I'll need to:
1. Add `agentTools` state for the agent's linked tools
2. Add `agentToolsLoading` and `agentToolsError` states
3. Fetch agent tools when the agent tab is selected or when component mounts


4. Update the Tools tab content to use `agentTools` instead of `tools`
5. Update the Add Tool dialog to show only tools not in `agentTools`

I'll modify the component to implement these changes, focusing on creating new states and updating the data fetching logic to support the new requirements.</details></think>

The changes are complete. Here's a summary of what I updated:

1. **Added new state variables** to separate agent-specific tools from all available tools:
   - `agentTools`, `agentToolsLoading`, `agentToolsError` - for tools linked to this specific agent
   - `allTools`, `allToolsLoading`, `allToolsError` - for all available tools (used in the dialog)

2. **Added a new useEffect** to fetch tools linked to this agent using the API:
   - `GET /agent-tools/agent/{agentUuid}/tools`

3. **Updated the Tools tab** to display `agentTools` (tools linked to this agent) instead of all tools

4. **Updated the Add Tool dialog** to:
   - Fetch all available tools from `/tools`
   - Filter out tools that are already linked to the agent (by checking if their UUID exists in `agentTools`)
   - Show an appropriate message when no tools are available to add (either due to search filter or all tools being already added)

    },
  ];
+ 
+ type TabType = "agent" | "tools" | "evaluation" | "data-extraction" | "settings";
+ 
+ const validTabs: TabType[] = ["agent", "tools", "evaluation", "data-extraction", "settings"];
  
  export function AgentDetail({ agentUuid, onHeaderUpdate }: AgentDetailProps) {
+   const searchParams = useSearchParams();
+   const router = useRouter();
+   
+   // Get initial tab from URL or default to "agent"
+   const getInitialTab = (): TabType =&gt; {
+     const tabParam = searchParams.get("tab");
+     if (tabParam &amp;&amp; validTabs.includes(tabParam as TabType)) {
+       return tabParam as TabType;
+     }
+     return "agent";
+   };
+ 
    const [agent, setAgent] = useState&lt;AgentData | null&gt;(null);
    const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    const [error, setError] = useState&lt;string | null&gt;(null);
-   const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState&lt;
-     "agent" | "tools" | "evaluation" | "data-extraction" | "settings"
-   &gt;("agent");
+   const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState&lt;TabType&gt;(getInitialTab);
+ 
+   // Update URL when tab changes
+   const handleTabChange = (tab: TabType) =&gt; {
+     setActiveTab(tab);
+     const params = new URLSearchParams(searchParams.toString());
+     params.set("tab", tab);
+     router.push(&#96;?${params.toString()}&#96;, { scroll: false });
+   };
    const [systemPrompt, setSystemPrompt] = useState("");
    const [sttProvider, setSttProvider] = useState&lt;string&gt;("");

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
No linter errors. The changes are complete. Now:
1. When a user clicks on a tab, the URL will update to include `?tab=<tabname>`
2. When the page is reloaded, the tab will be read from the URL query parameter and the correct tab will be displayed</details></think>

---

The changes are complete. Here's what I updated:

1. **Added imports** for `useSearchParams` and `useRouter` from `next/navigation`

2. **Created a `TabType` type** and `validTabs` array to ensure type safety

3. **Added `getInitialTab` function** that reads the `tab` query parameter from the URL on initial load

4. **Created `handleTabChange` function** that:
   - Updates the `activeTab` state
   - Updates the URL with the new tab parameter (e.g., `?tab=tools`)
   - Uses `{ scroll: false }` to prevent scrolling to top on tab change

5. **Updated all tab button onClick handlers** to use `handleTabChange` instead of `setActiveTab`

Now when you:
- Click on a tab → URL updates to `/agents/{uuid}?tab=tools` (or whichever tab)
- Reload the page → The same tab will be shown based on the URL parameter
- Share a link with a tab parameter → The recipient will see the correct tab

+                       &lt;button
+                         key={tool.uuid}

+                           setSelectedToolsForAdd((prev) =&gt; {
+                             const newSet = new Set(prev);

+                               newSet.delete(tool.uuid);
+                             } else {
+                               newSet.add(tool.uuid);
+                             }
+                             return newSet;
+                           });

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
No linter errors. The changes are complete. Now when the user opens the Add Tool dialog:
1. They can click on tools to select/deselect them (with a checkbox visual indicator)
2. Selected tools have a highlighted border3. When one or more tools are selected, an "Add (N)" button appears in the footer4. The selection is cleared when the dialog is closed (via backdrop click or Add new tool button)</details></think>

---

The changes are complete. Here's what I updated:

1. **Added a new state variable** `selectedToolsForAdd` to track selected tools as a `Set<string>` of tool UUIDs

2. **Updated tool buttons** to:
   - Toggle selection on click instead of closing the dialog
   - Show a checkbox indicator on the left side
   - Highlight selected tools with a different border color

3. **Updated the footer** to:
   - Show an "Add (N)" button when one or more tools are selected, displaying the count
   - Changed "Add new tool" button styling to be secondary (border style)
   - Added gap between buttons

4. **Clear selection** when the dialog is closed (via backdrop click, Add new tool, or Add button)

Now users can:
- Click to select multiple tools (checkboxes appear)
- See how many tools are selected in the "Add (N)" button
- Click "Add" to add all selected tools at once
- Click "Add new tool" to create a new tool (closes dialog)
